entry.title: Re: Lock Some fields of PR through ABAP Code
entry.content:
Did you already look at BAdI ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST, field selection methods such as FIELDSELECTION_HEADER allow to change some display field attributes. You may not be allowed to handle standard fields, but you could rise an error and restore previous value in PROCESS_HEADER.

  • More options/fields are handled in ME_PROCESS_REQ, but only SAP can implement it, so you would be required to use (divert) some standard SAP implementing class 🙃 (enhancement in an implementing class)
  • There were also some customer exits available (look for EXIT_SAPLMEREQ_*  FM in Enhancement MEREQ001)

entry.title: Re: Background RFC
entry.content:
@AbhishekSharma wrote:

once you call "START_OF_BACKGROUNDTASK" it will crate another WP which will run in Background mode.

That's wrong, it still runs in a dialog work process (START_OF_BACKGROUNDTASK is just to schedule the start of an RFC unit at a given date and time). Any ABAP code running in RFC runs in a dialog work process.

entry.title: B2B Integration Factory: What is Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content, and what is this used for?
entry.content:
The article B2B Integration Factory services to unlock the power of SAP Integration Suite's B2B capabilities mentions the regular creation, management, and delivery of pre-packaged B2B integration content. This blog is about the content of the Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content, why we now regularly deliver packages, and what the benefits are for users.

 

Introduction

We, the B2B Integration Factory, now regularly offer new and enhanced Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub à Community Section for two following reasons:

  1. Compared to SAP Cloud Integration or SAP PI/PO, the new B2B capabilities of SAP Integration Suite follow a new paradigm. This new paradigm focuses on the business-orientated, semantic view while creating B2B-related integration content. The technically oriented runtime artifacts will be automatically generated from it. The following chapter Business Oriented View for B2B Integration Content Building Describes this paradigm in more detail. This pre-packaged B2B integration content can be used as templates and examples to understand and internalize this new paradigm better.
  2. This pre-packaged B2B Integration Content also represents common B2B scenarios for electronic business transactions widely used in specific industries. We identified that the degree of reuse is very high when a similar B2B scenario is prevalent in a comparable or related industry. This makes it much more efficient for you to copy and adapt it accordingly for your local tenant.

Business Oriented View for B2B Integration Content Building

The Trading Partner Agreements (TPAs) of SAP's Trading Partner Management consider B2B scenarios in which the choreography of business documents is exchanged by the agreements with the respective business partners so that the agreed collaborative business process between the involved trading partners will be successfully fulfilled. The Integration Advisor then describes the APIs of these business documents, their behavior, usage, and especially implementation rules via functional specifications, the so-called Message Implementation Guidelines (MIGs). How a source MIG and corresponding target MIG are mapped on business meaning and syntactic levels are then described in assigned Mapping Guidelines. Here, the Trading Partner Management and the Integration Advisor align to the conventions of the ISO/IEC 14662 standard "Information Technologies - Open-EDI reference model." the figure shown divides the electronic exchange of Business Data into a Business Operational View (BOV) and a Functional Service View (FSV). 7

The BOV considers the semantics of business data in business transactions and the associated data exchange that applies to business requirements between business partners. The BOV-related specifications and rules shape B2B Scenarios in the respective domains. The intention is to shape TPAs, especially the choreography of the Business Transactions in the B2B Scenario, which should align with the concepts of UMM (UN/CEFACT Modeling Methodology).

The FSV deals with the supporting services that meet the technical requirements for the runtime execution of electronic business data exchange and focuses on information technology aspects such as functional capabilities, service interfaces, technical mapping artifacts, and exchange protocols. These technical runtime artifacts will be automatically generated from the definitions and configurations in the Trading Partner Agreements and stored in the central Partner Directory, which will be dynamically obtained according to the received business data on the sender side.

In other words, the supported approach by Trading Partner Management and Integration Advisor gives you significant advantages. As a business-oriented B2B expert, you must focus on the requirements on a business-oriented level while designing trading partner agreements. Trading Partner Management and Integration Advisor then ensure the technical artifacts are automatically created from these business-oriented definitions and configurations.

What do you find in a Pre-Packaged B2B Integration Content?

The pre-packaged B2B integration content follows the paradigm of the business-oriented view. This includes:

Trading Partner Profile (TPP)

A sample Trading Partner Profile (TPP) and the corresponding sample Company Profile (CPP) with the essential information required for the trading partner agreement, such as

  • Primary overview data
  • Example identifiers
  • Example business context
  • B2B System with a required type system, such as UN/EDIFACT, and example communication channels for end-to-end simulation via an API testing tool.
  • Custom parameters, if necessary
  • Predefined number ranges

 

Trading Partner Agreement + Agreement Template

A Trading Partner Agreement (TPA) and the corresponding Agreement Template with a B2B scenario to fulfill a collaborative business process in a specific industry using electronic data interchange by the B2B Capabilities at Integration Suite.

This Trading Partner Agreement also considers a specific type of system for the company (e.g., SAP IDoc) and another particular Type System and its version for the trading partner (e.g., UN/EDIFACT D.96A).

In addition, this B2B scenario involves a choreography of one-way and two-way business transactions required to fulfill the desired collaborative business process.

Each one-way and two-way business transaction consists of Business Transaction Activities that refer to the required source and target MIGs according to their direction.

Furthermore, each Business Transaction Activity contains a predefined Mapping Guideline (MAG) that describes the mapping between the corresponding source and target MIGs.

Message Implementation Guidelines (MIG)

All the assigned MIGs cover the required semantics and structure for the given business context in which the MIG is used. The business context is a combination of the set of classifications in which the MIG is considered, such as industry, country, or trading partner role. It also covers the context of the Business Transaction Activity in which the MIG is used. These MIGs also have a meaningful description of the scope, use, and conditions.

All the MIGs used in the TPAs have a precise and consistent qualification based on the IA-supported qualification approach. This powerful qualification approach not only helps to declare the business meaning of like in the following figure shown semantically individual groups such as “Buyer” or “Supplier” that are derived from a generic group called “SG2 - Name and Address.” It also helps to make the mapping elements in MAGs much more straightforward and will be automatically considered in the validation rules.

Furthermore, all defined nodes in the MIGs have meaningful names, descriptions, and correct properties such as cardinality, primitive type, length, format, etc. Coded leaf nodes refer to the required code lists, which might be restricted if only a subset of the code values is needed.

If necessary, integrity conditions and business rules across nodes are expressed via assertions for further validation.

Mapping Guidelines (MAG)

Each Mapping Guideline is based on the associated source and target MIGs built as described in the previous chapter. Each MAG also meaningfully describes the scope, use, and conditions and has all the relevant mapping elements between the semantically corresponding source and target nodes.

Each mapping element exists of one or more of the following characteristics:

  • The primary use of the embedded no-coding features, if possible. For example, the mapping elements between date/time leaf nodes are based on the embedded “Date Time Conversion,” in which the leaf nodes' given date/time format must be set.
  • With XPath V2.0-based mapping element functions when the embedded no-coding features do not support the criteria. XPath 2.0 is a logically constructed, compelling, easy-to-learn language and a powerful function library that can be used for nearly all transformation requirements.
  • Code value mappings based on the embedded code value mapping logic.
  • Conditions that are based on the built-in condition logic.
  • Meaningful descriptions and notes about this mapping element's intention, behavior, and conditions.

These provided MAGs don’t have many if-then-else statements because the consequent use of the mentioned qualification approach has already resolved most of them. Some MAGs might have pre-transformation rules based on the embedded pre-transformation logic. This is especially required if the order of the nodes in the source payload must be sorted into the expected structure so this can be mapped to the target structure.

The behavior of these Mapping Guidelines can be directly simulated with the example values in the source MIGs.

Pre- Post-Processing Integration Flows

Some of the requirements the MAG can directly express some of the requirements. ,.Target sorting may be necessary, some tag names must be substituted, or an additional call to an external source must be made. For this purpose, you will find in the pre-packaged B2B integration content the necessary pre- or post-post-processing integration flows in a separate integration package. These pre- or post-processing integration flows are directly assigned to the appropriate Business Transaction Activity of the given Trading Partner Agreement.

How do you get and activate the Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content?

You’ll find it in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. You can discover this by searching for “B2B Integration Factory”. Apart from the listed B2B-related Integration Packages with your custom extensions, you’ll also find a list of available Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content for diverse B2B scenarios in which an electronic business data exchange between different type systems is required. You can then download the appropriate archive (zip) files in GitHub and import, activate, and test it. , as well as how to use the guide. The attached configuration and how to use the guide describe all the required steps.

All the pre-packaged B2B integration content is community content provided as a standalone component under Apache License, v.2.0. It is not part of any SAP product. Therefore, it can be locally adjusted according to the final requirements. SAP does not provide official support for all the B2B integration content offered. If you find an issue in the provided content, please raise any issue under the associated GitHub project by clicking the “Issues” button.

If you miss anything in the provided pre-packaged B2B integration content for a specific B2B scenario, you can also pull a request from GitHub via the button “Pull Request.” As the B2B Integration Factory, we regularly check all the requests and shape our future backlog according to the demand we also find here.

entry.title: Seeking Support to Achieve My Dream: SAP HCM Associate Certificate
entry.content:
As an aspiring SAP HCM professional, I am on a journey to deepen my skills and knowledge in the world of SAP. Over the past year, I have transitioned from an SAP ABAP developer role into an SAP HCM consultant position. This shift has allowed me to explore the intricate workings of Human Capital Management and develop hands-on experience with SAP's comprehensive solutions.

In my current role, I have gained valuable experience in understanding the complexities of SAP HCM, such as personnel administration, organizational management, and time management. However, to further my professional growth and truly solidify my foundation, I am keen on earning the SAP HCM Associate Certification. Achieving this certification would not only validate my skills but also open new opportunities for career advancement and allow me to contribute more effectively to my organization.

Unfortunately, due to financial constraints, I am unable to afford the cost of the certification. I am reaching out to the SAP community and certification bodies to seek support for obtaining this valuable credential. I believe that with a certification, I can maximize my potential and continue contributing to SAP HCM projects with confidence and expertise.

Why Am I a Worthy Candidate?

  • Passion for SAP HCM: I am passionate about leveraging SAP technology to optimize HR processes and improve organizational efficiency. I consistently invest time in self-learning through available online resources, webinars, and hands-on practice.

  • Practical Experience: With my background as an SAP ABAP developer and my current role as an SAP HCM consultant, I have acquired a blend of technical and functional skills. This combination enables me to approach SAP HCM problems with a holistic perspective, bridging the gap between technical development and business requirements.

  • Commitment to Professional Growth: Despite financial challenges, I remain dedicated to advancing my career and skill set. The SAP HCM certification would serve as a catalyst for my professional development and would enable me to deliver better outcomes for clients and projects.

How Can SAP and the Community Support Me?

I am requesting assistance in the form of a scholarship or sponsorship to cover the certification cost. By investing in me, SAP or any supporting organization would not only be helping an individual achieve their goals but also contributing to the growth of the SAP community by enabling a committed professional to flourish.

Thank you for taking the time to read my story. I remain hopeful that I will find a way to achieve this certification and continue my journey as an SAP HCM consultant. Your support would be greatly appreciated, and I promise to pay it forward by helping others in similar situations once I am in a position to do so.

Sincerely,
Yeswanthraj Krishnan.

entry.title: Re: Converting DOCX file to PDF?
entry.content:
I think that only way would be using some third party converter application.

For example you can think about installing some "converter library" on application server and calling it via the SM69 external command in ABAP. Plus of course saving DOCX & reading converted PDF files through OPEN DATASET commands on application server.

entry.title: Extract KNKK Customer Master Credit Management (Control Area) Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP Program -  Z_RKDKLI_KNKK_CUSTOMER           

  •  Program Type – ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_RKDKLI_KNKK_CUSTOMER    
  • Tcode - Z_KNKK_CUST    
  • Entity Description - Customer Master Credit Management (Control Area) 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- RKDKLI_KNKK.CSV  
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – KNKK, KNB1 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  
  • KUNNR 
  • KLIMK  

---------------ABAP Program start from here-------------------

REPORT Z_RKDKLI_KNKK_CUSTOMER.

TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.

PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\KNKK.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.

START-OF-SELECTION.

*&------WRITE: / 'LEGAL ENTITY NAME:', ENTITY.

*&------WRITE: / 'OUTPUT FILE PATH:', OPATH.

type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
KUNNR like KNKK-KUNNR,
KLIMK like KNKK-KLIMK,
BUKRS like KNB1-BUKRS,
end of itab.

data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
SELECT DISTINCT KNKK~KUNNR
KNKK~KLIMK
KNB1~BUKRS
from KNKK INNER JOIN KNB1 ON KNKK~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.

CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: SCIM 2.0 API - User Provisioning - Bulk Operations (BulkRequest)
entry.content:
Hi All,

Recently started investigating SCIM 2.0 API functionality for Datasphere. I managed to create a single user, as well as delete a user. So authorization wise I should not encounter any issues.

Now for bulk operations following scheme needs to be used: 

"schemas": ["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:BulkRequest"]

Running this for both the creation of users as well as for the deletion of users, it doens't seem to work. I received a 400 error ("Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema").

 
Looking a bit deeper into the list of all avaible schemas under ../scim2/schemas, I don't see the BulkRequest schema listed.
 
I did check the /scim2/ServiceProviderConfig and bulk is supported: 
"bulk":{"supported":true,"maxOperations":30,"maxPayloadSize":3072000}
 
Anyone managed to use this BulkRequest schema and if so how?
Thanks in advance!
 
Kind Regards,
Thibault

entry.title: GSR Journal Entry is Posting to the Balance Sheet Account Instead of the Expense Account
entry.content:
We've observed that some Goods and Service Receipts are being recorded in the balance sheet account (Deferred Cost of Goods Sold) instead of the expense account (COGS). We've reviewed all configurations, and everything appears to be set up correctly.

The issue is that some GSRs are hitting Deferred COGS while others are going to COGS.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GSR Journal Entry is Posting to the Balance Sheet Acc...
entry.content:
Kindly check the Account Assignment sub-tab in the Items tab of the respective Goods and Services Receipt.

When you choose Account Assignment Type "Sales Order", the expense is immediately assigned to the sales order item and therefore posted to the respective expense account.

When you use Account Assignment Type "Project Task", the expense is then posted as Deferred Cost of Goods sold and assigned to the respective sales order item separately during the Expense Assignment posting.

entry.title: Extract EDPAR Convert External < > Internal Partner Number Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP Program - Z_EDPAR_CUSTOMER 

  • Program Type – ABAP  
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_EDPAR_CUSTOMER 
  • Tcode - Z_ EDPAR _CUST
  • Entity Description - Customer Master Sales Data 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- EDPAR.CSV 
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – EDPAR, KNA1, KNB1 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  
  • KUNNR 
  • MANDT 
  • PARVW 
  • EXPNR 
  • INPNR 

----------------ABAP PROGRAM START FROM HERE-------------------------

 

REPORT  Z_EDPAR_CUSTOMER.
 
TYPE-POOLS: VRM.
 
DATA: NAME  TYPE VRM_ID,
      LIST  TYPE VRM_VALUES,
      VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.
 
 
PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\EDPAR.csv'.
 
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
 
NAME = 'ENTITY'.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.
START-OF-SELECTION.
*&------WRITE: / 'LEGAL ENTITY NAME:', ENTITY.
*&------WRITE: / 'OUTPUT FILE PATH:', OPATH.
type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
      KUNNR like EDPAR-KUNNR,
  MANDT like EDPAR-MANDT,
  PARVW like EDPAR-PARVW,
  EXPNR like EDPAR-EXPNR,
   INPNR like EDPAR-INPNR,
   BUKRS like KNB1-BUKRS,
      end of itab.
data:  itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
  select  EDPAR~KUNNR
EDPAR~MANDT
EDPAR~PARVW
EDPAR~EXPNR
   EDPAR~INPNR
                 KNB1~BUKRS
         FROM EDPAR INNER JOIN KNA1 ON EDPAR~KUNNR = KNA1~KUNNR INNER JOIN KNB1 ON EDPAR~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR
 
         into table itab
         WHERE EDPAR~PARVW = 'AG' AND KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.
 
CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
 EXPORTING
   I_FIELD_SEPERATOR          = ','
  TABLES
    I_TAB_SAP_DATA             = itab
 CHANGING
   I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA       =  itab1
 EXCEPTIONS
   CONVERSION_FAILED          = 1
   OTHERS                     = 2
          .
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
 MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
         WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.
 
   CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      filename = OPATH
    TABLES
      data_tab = itab1
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS   = 1.

entry.title: MS Excel Export from SALV
entry.content:
When I use the standard function in SALV (CL_SALV_TABLE) for MS Excel export, an Excel file with multiple worksheets is opened. The worksheets “Format”, “Header”, and “RawData” are each filled with the same data. What do I need to do to have only one worksheet? Here is the code:

 

 

  METHOD salv.

    CONSTANTS: lc_land_it TYPE land1 VALUE 'IT'.

    " Data selection.
    SELECT spras, land1, landx
      FROM t005t
        WHERE land1 = @LC_land_it
      INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_data).

    " Genrate SALV object.
    cl_salv_table=>factory(
      IMPORTING r_salv_table = DATA(lr_salv)
      CHANGING  t_table      = lt_data ).

    " Basic settings
    lr_salv->get_functions( )->set_all( abap_true ).
    lr_salv->get_columns( )->set_optimize( abap_true ).
    lr_salv->get_display_settings( )->set_list_header( 'Überschrift'(001) ).
    lr_salv->get_display_settings( )->set_striped_pattern( abap_true ).
    lr_salv->get_selections( )->set_selection_mode( if_salv_c_selection_mode=>row_column ).

    " Display SALV.
    lr_salv->display( ).

  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: Containerizing SAP S/4HANA Systems with Docker
entry.content:
Dear AndrewGugger,

Thank you for sharing this insightful post! It's an exciting development, and I understand we are still in the early stages of this journey with many more steps ahead before we can use this idea in our daily routines. But I would like some clarifications, if possible.

In terms of the N+M DEV/QAS/PROD landscape suggested above, what could be realistic/ideal applications for containers in contrast to VMs? I mean, in terms, of High Availability for Systems and some Components (Router, WebDispatcher etc) as well Scalable/On-Demand launch of new Instances is a common use. But what about resources and customer data?

Can we really achieve a lightweight S/4HANA ERP DEV System? What you show above with the DEV instances could be done with Non-ERP Systems, as is the case of the Docker ABAP Development Trial appliance (~32GB), but with a fulll S/4HANA ERP System with hundreds of GB of memory requirement is it really doable to launch N instances of a DEV System? Especially considering the cost per minute of an AWS instance, for example?

You mentioned using SWPM to create new/updated images. How would that work in practice for existing Systems with loads of data in terms of SPS and Release Upgrades? Could we see a case where SAP would use SWPM to create and publish new base images, that the customers could then use to create their own images with SWPM and/or SUM? What about the persistence layers, and the compatibility of image upgrades with persisted customer Data?

Since right now SGEN and Customer Data share the same DB, Could we see a case where we have split DBs, one for system and one for customer data, where updated SGEN DBs could be continuously delivered via images, leaving the customer DB always untouched? Is Data migration a more viable alternative in this case? Or would gCTS be ultimately responsible for orchestrating the integration of new images into existing SAP Systems?

Do you know of any other blog posts with a more in-depth discussion on how this could be optimally performed? 

I appreciate any further thoughts you (or any other member) can provide on these points. Thank you again for your valuable insights.

Best regards,

Eloi Bermejo

entry.title: Custom ABAP Class using SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1
entry.content:
Hello,

I thought of creating a custom class in order to call the function 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1' to read the SOFM text from a user's comment and then implement it as text in an email notification task.
I don't want to create a custom object and add this method inside for now so I thought of creating an ABAP Class and calling the method from here.
The code I used is:

method EXTRACT.
DATA: lt_object_content TYPE TABLE OF soli,
lv_document_text TYPE string,
ls_content TYPE soli.

" Call the function module to extract the text from the SOFM object
CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
EXPORTING
document_id = iv_document_id
TABLES
object_content = lt_object_content
EXCEPTIONS
no_authorization = 1
document_not_found = 2
others = 3.

" Check for the return value of the function module
IF sy-subrc = 0.
" Concatenate the text into a single string (using APPEND for better performance)
LOOP AT lt_object_content INTO ls_content.
" Append the line to the final text variable
lv_document_text = lv_document_text && ls_content-line && cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline.
ENDLOOP.

" Set the extracted text to the output parameter
rv_document_text = lv_document_text.

ELSE.
" Handle error scenario with more specific messages
CASE sy-subrc.
WHEN 1.
rv_document_text = 'No authorization to read the document'.
WHEN 2.
rv_document_text = 'Document not found'.
WHEN OTHERS.
rv_document_text = 'Error reading SOFM object'.
ENDCASE.
ENDIF.

endmethod.

with two insert and export parameters 
import: IV_DOCUMENT_ID
export: RV_DOCUMENT_TEXT
I dont know if this code works because it is still in process mode when I test, the status doesnt go in completion (yes its a background job, I think a terminating event has to be raised).
What are all the necessary steps I should do? thank you very much in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ANS - Is WSDL webservice supported in actions?
entry.content:
Hi SandeepRenuka,

Although we have never tried it, on theory you should be able to call your SOAP service by using webhook actions
However keep in mind the following:
1. Only "No authentication", "Basic Authentication" and "OAuth authentication" is supported
2. You will probably need to utilize "Payload Template" field to build your XML payload from the event
3. You will probably need to specify "Headers" field to "content-type: application/xml".

Please try to setup the webhook action to your SOAP service and update this question with the results. If you encounter any problems we can try to help further.

entry.title: Re: I am having trouble trying to create a program in SE3...
entry.content:
Luis, 

Since you're on a RISE system, this could be related to your proxy settings. I have already faced the same issue. To resolve it, please follow the steps outlined in SAP Note 3320366, specifically the section titled "Program execution procedure (with proxy)". This will guide you through creating an RFC connection and obtaining the necessary Middleware server GUID then don't forgot to adjust the data in /MERP/CORE_CPMS_APPCREATE_PROG.

These steps should resolve the HTTP connection issue you're currently facing. However, you might encounter other issues during the application creation process. If this happens, don't worry! There are likely existing SAP Notes that address these issues. You can search for these notes on the SAP Support Portal using relevant keywords or error messages.

entry.title: S/4 Hana Upgrades: Process of Cloning Tables in ZDO
entry.content:
The Process of Cloning Tables in ZDO

The process of creating table clones happens when the end-users have been rolled over to the bridge subsystem. Before the process of cloning can be started, Software Update Manager has to prepare the existing tables used by the source release (V1). The preparation includes renaming tables from the original name to a temporary name. This ensures that the target version (V2) of the table can be created with the original name.

The rename is called smart-switch and happens in the phase EU_SWITCH_ZDM. Here is a pseudo code describing what the phase EU_SWITCH_ZDM does:

  • Acquires an exclusive table lock for table tab1
  • Renames table tab1 to tab1temp
  • Releases the exclusive table lock for table tab1
  • Smart-switching tables require an exclusive lock for tables on database level. In case of frequently updated tables (approx. more than 10 changes / second), this can be difficult in peak load hours. Software Update Manager Toolbox offers the tool DB Table Lock analyser to identify suitable timeframes to plan the smart switch in.

Step 1 of the Process of Cloning Tables in ZDO

 

  • The initial transfer describes the process of creating the initial clone of the table with the same structure.
  • Software Update Manager executes the initial transfer in the phase SQLRUNTASK_FDCT_TRANSFER.
  • The transfer happens using native database tools.
  • For SAP HANA®, the tables are cloned with the statement CREATE TABLE tab(V2) LIKE tab(V1) WITH DATA.
  • From this time, all changes happening on version 1 of the table is captured in an associated logging table.
  • Capturing the changes in the logging table is happening by database triggers that have been created in the table version 1 before the initial transfer was started.

Step 2 of the Process of Cloning Tables in ZDO

 

  • The process of capturing changes in the logging table continues until the replay of the changes in phase RUN_ZDOREPLAY_REPLAY_LKPM starts.
  • Here, Software Update Manager switches the way how changes are captured.
  • The database trigger from table version 1 to the logging table stops capturing changes.
  • From this time, the changes done in table version 1 are synchronously forwarded to table version 2 by another database trigger.
  • All changes captured in the logging table are replayed into table version 2.
  • Replaying the changes and switching over to synchronously replication ensures transactional consistency.

Step 3 of the Process of Cloning Tables in ZDO

 

  • When the replay in the phase RUN_ZDOREPLAY_REPLAY_LKPM is finished, the logging table is no longer used.
  • The upgrade continues with the remaining activities such as adjusting the table version 2 to the target structure, importing table content, or executing application-defined procedures on table version 2.

entry.title: Changeable Sales orders for Solution Orders
entry.content:
When you create a solution order with sales items and release them, the system automatically creates a sales order. Previously, these were display-only sales orders of type SOR, and you couldn't change them. Now, the system behavior has changed. When you release the sales item, changeable sales orders of type OR (standard orders) are created by default.

As a solution order specialist, you previously faced limitations when trying to edit and fulfill sales orders created from solution orders. Editing these sales orders was not possible directly. However, this process has now improved. You now have the flexibility to modify sales items created from solution orders directly, simplifying the process and saving time.

When you release a sales item in a solution order, the system forwards all its data to create a changeable sales order. Any additional changes must be made in the sales orders. You can navigate to the follow-up sales order to make necessary changes.  Rejection code, schedule lines, delivery & billing status, and plant are updated back to solution orders. The changes in the follow-up sales orders are indicated in solution orders.

The changes are also displayed in the change log, which is available in the sales item detail view. You can view the sync status in the change log to determine whether the changed field is synced to the solution order or not.

For more information on changeable sales order, you can refer to the video Changeable Sales Orders for Solution Orders.

Note:

  • The sales item created from solution orders can’t be deleted.
  •  You can’t change the product ID.
  •  Incomplete sales orders can’t be created from solution orders. 

 Now let’s talk about some examples where you can use changeable sales orders.

Example 1: Handling Freight Cost

Let’s assume a customer ordered packaging solution. During the fulfilment of the sales order, the freight cost is determined. As sales orders created from solution orders are editable now, the internal sales representative updates the price in the sales order to reflect the additional freight costs and generated the order confirmation. The solution order specialist noticed that the sales order item had been updated in the solution orders to include these added freight costs.

Example 2: Navigating Stock Issues with Third-Party Shipping

Imagine a scenario where a solution order specialist released a solution order for fulfillment, but a delay occurred because a part of the solution is out of stock. To mitigate the delay, the sales order representative converted the item category to accommodate the purchase process, which initiated a direct shipment to the customer from supplier. The solution order specialist received the updated notification in solution orders.

Example 3: Ensuring On-Time Delivery with Adjusted Shipping Conditions

Let’s assume for another solution, the solution order specialist scheduled the order using the standard shipping conditions based on the customer master data.  However, the customer later requested an urgent delivery. The internal sales update the shipping condition to speed up the delivery process. The solution order specialist could view this change on both the item list and the item details page and use the information to adjust the installation service schedule accordingly.

Example 4:  Applying Discounts after the Quote is Accepted

After the quote was accepted and the solution order was processed for fulfilment, the customer requested a special first-time discount upon reviewing the order confirmation. The solution order specialist confirmed the sales part had been sent for fulfilment but not billed. To prevent premature billing, a billing block was set, the discount manually applied, and the updated order confirmation verified. The billing block was then released to proceed with billing based on the revised pricing, which altered the net value of the sales items.

Activating Changeable Sales Orders

  • If you already use scope item Solution Order (4GT), note that the creation of display-only sales orders of type SOR is deprecated and will be deactivated with an upcoming release. Raise a ticket to the application component: CRM-S4-SOL-SLO to activate the creation of changeable sales orders of type OR (standard orders).
  • If you haven’t used the scope item Solution Order (4GT) before and you activate it now, the system creates changeable sales orders of type OR (standard orders) by default.

entry.title: INTRODUCTION TO GROUP REPORTING IN SAP S/4HANA (PART II)
entry.content:
MAIN CONFIGURATION AND MASTER DATA ELEMENTS

In this section there is a summary of the main tables, customizable objects and master data elements used in the consolidation processes within SAP Group reporting.

1. DATA MODEL

 a) SOURCE TABLES: The tables used to obtain the financial records for entities in SAP S/4HANA are:

  • Table ACDOCA: Universal Journal Entry Table. It contains the actual postings that will be transferred to consolidation table.
  • Table ACDOCP: Plan Data Line Items. It contains the plan postings that will be transferred to consolidation table.

 b) GROUP REPORTING TABLES: The tables used to maintain the Group reporting processes are:

  • Table ACDOCU: Universal Consolidation Journal Entries. It incorporates the consolidation records transferred from S/4HANA finance tables, collected from API or processed by manual postings.

This table has over hundred fields, the most important being: consolidation ledger, financial statement item, consolidation unit, transaction currency, local currency and group currency. The data is stored on periodic basis. 

  • Tables FINCS*: Configuration and Mapping Tables specific for Group Reporting. In S/4HANA 1909 there are over 70 tables available.

 

2. CUSTOMIZABLE OBJECTS

This section details the elements that are required to configure in the system through Customizing (Transaction Code SPRO) to run Group reporting processes. 

 a) CONSOLIDATION LEDGER: This element is defined by two main attributes:

  • Group currency: Determines the currency of the financial data of the consolidation groups. To consolidate in different currencies, it is required to create separate ledgers for each group currency.
  • Valuation related to a reference ledger: The reference ledger specifies the source ledger of the universal journal on the FI accounting side. The integrated values are taken from the reference ledger.

To define a consolidation ledger the following IMG path is accessed:

SAP delivers ledgers Y1 with the currency EUR and Y2 with the currency USD.

 

b) CONSOLIDATION DIMENSION: Organizational unit to proceed with consolidation.

To define or edit a consolidation dimension the following IMG path is accessed:

By default SAP delivers Y1 - Companies.

The default and minimum length of consolidation unit is 6, to consider external companies not included in SAP S/4HANA.

 

c) CONSOLIDATION VERSION: Identifies a specific data area in the consolidation database to fulfill different reporting needs. For example to separate actuals data for publishing official reports from planning and forecast data for internal evaluation.

To define a consolidation version the following IMG path is accessed:

SAP delivers several consolidation versions that can be adjusted or added new ones.

It is possible to assign special versions to retrieve of the maintained settings among different consolidation versions. Then, it is only needed to enter the control parameters once in special versions and assign the special versions to consolidation versions.

For example, consolidation versions for actuals data and planning data can share all special version settings except that for Data entry. In this case, you can assign different Data entry special versions but same other special versions to each consolidation version. When you perform a task in a consolidation version, the system reads the special versions that are relevant for that task and that are assigned to that consolidation version.

Find below a sample configuration for Budget Version for a given year:

Find also below the configuration for actual Version:

 

 d) BREAKDOWN CATEGORY: Classifies sub-assignments that are required for Financial Statement (FS) items to perform consolidation tasks. The following breakdown category fields are supported:

  • Partner Unit
  • Subitem Category
  • Subitem
  • Transaction Currency
  • Unit of Measure

For each FS item, the breakdown category determines which sub-assignments must be maintained.

To define a breakdown category the following IMG path is accessed:

SAP delivers several breakdown categories that can be adjusted or added new ones.

 

e) SUBITEM CATEGORY: Classifies the subitems, which are needed for the sub-assignment of Financial Statement items. A subitem category is assigned to the relevant FS items via breakdown categories.

SAP delivers two predefined subitem categories: Transaction Types and Functional areas.

To define a subitem category or subitem the following IMG path is accessed:

Each subitem category contains the possible subitems to be determined with the description of each of them. Subitems are used to further breakdown the values recorded on financial statement (FS) items. For example, transaction type 100 (opening balance – orig. cost) is used for balance carryforward. 

f) DOCUMENT TYPE: Identifies the type and source of data; i.e., reported data is stored on different document types than elimination data. Can be used in various consolidation settings, tasks and reports.

To define a document type the following IMG path is accessed:

 

Document types have various attributes assigned, including data sources (manual or automatic posting, file upload, or API), posting levels, deferred income tax handling, automatic reversal, and currencies to be used in posting.

 

g) TASK: Activities for collecting and preparing the financial data reported by the consolidation units in consolidation processes.

To define a task the following IMG path is accessed:

 

 h) TASK GROUP: Define the tasks included in Data Monitor and Consolidation Monitor, ensuring a logical sequence and consistent data.

To define a task group the following IMG path is accessed:

 The position assigned to the task represents the order in which the task will be displayed in the Monitor.

 3. MASTER DATA OBJECTS

This section details the master data elements that are maintained by the users to run Group reporting processes. 

a) FINANCIAL STATEMENT ITEM: Represents the fundamental account assignment in the consolidation system.

FS items are dependent on the consolidation chart of accounts. They are not only used for accounting purposes on balance sheets, P&L statements, or statements of retained earnings, but can also be used for maintaining metrics and key figures.

SAP pre-delivers a set of FS items, that can be edited or extended using the following Fiori App in the Launchpad:

This Fiori App has the capabilities to display the existing the Financial Statement Items considering the selections done in the filter area. From the list, it is possible to edit the selected FS item or create a new one.

 

The main characteristics to maintain are:

  • FS Type: Attribute that indicates the nature of FS items.
  • Breakdown Category: Mandatory or optional assignments to be determined while posting with the FS item.
  • Selection Attributes: Classification assigned to FS items so that all FS items that share the same attribute value can be selected in order to be treated in the same way, for example, in consolidation tasks.

Target Attributes: Designed to derive a FS item based on another FS item for specific purposes. If you use target attributes, you don't have to assign the FS items in the rule itself. Instead the system identifies FS items based on the master data. For example, for the FS item 111100-Cash on hand, you select the FS item 111200-Short term deposits for the elimination target attribute. 

The GL accounts relevant for consolidation purposes are required to be mapped with a FS item. The maintenance of that mapping is done acceding to the following Fiori App in the Launchpad:

This Fiori App has the capabilities to create a new mapping, edit/display the existing mapping or import the mapping from a template.

In case of importing the FS Item mapping, the system will show a pop-up to choose the file to upload it. In the same screen, there is the option to download the template to make sure the file is correctly populated.

In case of editing or displaying the mapping, the system will display all the GL accounts already mapped with a FS item and the GL accounts pending to map to be reviewed.

 b) CONSOLIDATION UNIT: Lowest organization level for consolidation.  

The consolidation units can be created, edited or display using the following Fiori Apps in the Launchpad:

The following settings will be defined for each consolidation unit:

  • Tax Rate: Default tax rate applicable in postings.
  • Local Currency
  • Translation Method: determines the exchange rate used for specific financial statement (FS) items to translate the local currency to the group currency, and how translation and rounding differences are posted.
  • Data Transfer Method: Specifies how the local reported financial data is collected for consolidatiod. Having the option to choose one of the following methods:
    • Read from Universal Document: applicable for consolidation units whose reported financial data is handled in the S/4HANA system.
    • Flexible Upload

 c) CONSOLIDATION GROUP: Structured list of consolidation units. A consolidation unit can belong to one or more consolidation groups.

The consolidation groups can be created, edited or display using the following Fiori Apps in the Launchpad:

The following settings will be defined for each consolidation group:

  • Ledger: Consolidation Ledger using for processing the consolidation unit.
  • Group Currency: Determined by the consolidation ledger.
  • Method: Validation methods assigned to the consolidation group.

 Once the consolidation groups are defined, the consolidation units are assigned to them using the following Fiori Apps in the Launchpad:

While assigning the consolidation unit, is required to define the type of consolidation method that will be used and the periods of assignment to the consolidation group.

 

 d) VALIDATION RULE: Condition to verify data correctness according to basic financial principles, such as total assets must equal the sum of the total liabilities plus the sum of total equity.

The defined validation rules can be used to verify reported data, standardized data, and consolidated data along the consolidation process. They are  defined using the following Fiori App in the Launchpad:

 

 

While defining rules, the following parameters are maintained:

  • Rule expression: Comprised of two formulas and a comparison operator. A formula can include fixed amounts, constants, mathematical operators and year-to-date amounts posted with a range of FS items.
  • Tolerance settings: Tolerance value or tolerance percentage, so that the difference between the calculated values between both formulas can be ignored as long as it does not exceed the tolerance.
  • Control level: Specifies how strict the rule is. That means, if a failed validation results to an error, warning or information status for the respective validation task in monitors.
  • Group-by field: Specifies the grouping criteria of the data validated by the rule in the way that will be organized on the validation result screen.
  • Comments required: Select the checkbox if comments are needed to provide further explanation for the validation result.

 e) VALIDATION METHOD: Set of validation rules to verify data correctness.

The validation methods can be created and assign to them multiple defined rules using the following Fiori App in the Launchpad:

SAP pre-delivers the validation methods SRD1 (Local Data Validation Method) and SCD1 (Consolidated Data Validation Method) that contain a set of predefined rules. It is possible to create new methods in the customer namespace: IDs not starting with the prefix S.

Once the validation methods are defined, they can be assigned to consolidation groups or consolidation units depending on the task selected: reported data validation, standardized data validation or consolidated data validation. The assignment is done in the following Fiori App:

 

First, the desired task will be selected. For the 01 and 02 task types, the validation method is assigned to consolidation units, while for the 03 task type, the validation method is assigned to consolidation groups.

 

In the assignment screen, the validation method is activated for the desired periods and consolidations units/groups.

entry.title: Is it a fact that matching our skills on our resumes can make it easier to find jobs on career site?
entry.content:
Are you still searching the Jobs using keywords or location in the career site to find them?  Why not make use of AI-assistance skills matching to find the appropriate Job on the career site

Everyone in the fast-paced world is searching for jobs on career sites, SAP SuccessFactors recruiting marketing offers the search results page on displays the option to upload a resume with the help of AI-Assisted skills Matching for Resumes.

The Unified Data Model Career Site Builder initiative has resulted in Skills Matching panel looks like on the search results page in career sites.

Prerequisites:

  • The Career Site Builder is enabled by the Unified Data Model.

Find out how to enable SuccessFactors Recruiting for new career sites, which includes job search and layout choices that can be manipulated through the Unified Data Model. https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/successfactors-recruiting-new...

  • To opt-in this feature, the AI units license has been purchased. For more information about AI units license, please contact your Account Executive.

Let's discuss two important aspects of integrating AI-assisted job skills on career sites and AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Candidate Resumes

1. AI-assisted job skills on career sites:

Using a Skills Cloud component in Career Site Builder, you can display job skills on your career site in the form of a word cloud.

Below is an illustration of the Skills Cloud component on the job details page. (Colors can be configured in the style settings for your brand.)

This feature was developed to aid candidates in identifying the necessary skills for a job and visualize how their skills meet the job requirements.                                                                                                  

2.AI-assisted Skills Matching for Candidate Resumes:

Applicants can upload their resume direct within the Skills Cloud component on the job details page, but only if the AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Resumes feature has been enabled.

This is how the Skills Matching panel appears on the search results page and Click on Get started

Note: The style settings for your brand have the ability to configure colors. 

Appearance  Styles  <Brand>  Search Experience  Skills Matching

Appearance  Styles  <Brand>  Search Experience  Skills Cloud

Select the country/Region: To proceed with skill matching, please accept the consent statement.

Before uploading it, the candidate can be presented with a data privacy consent statement (DPCS).  Then, we recommend jobs that match their skills.

Once the DPCS is accepted and their resume is uploaded, we use AI to evaluate the resume and identify their abilities.

AI provides recommendations for jobs that are aligned with their skills and you can select the appropriate jobs to apply for.

After selecting the appropriate job, the job description page shows your relevant skills and overriding skills for the job you are applying for.

Just bearing in mind the following points:

  • The resume is only used temporarily to identify the candidate's skills and suggest suitable jobs. 
  • The resume and skill set are only kept for 30 minutes and are not linked to a job application in any way. 
  • When a candidate decides to apply, they are required to upload their resume once more.

SuccessFactors and CSB require setup of configuration steps:

  1. Enable Assisted Job Skills for Career Site Builder on the AI Services Administration page, so that new job postings, in the future, are analyzed to extract skills from the job description, using AI.
  2. Run the scheduled job Extract Skills from Job Description Using AI on the Scheduled Job Manager page, so that existing job postings, already posted, are analyzed to extract skills from the job description, using AI.
  3. The Skills Cloud component can be added to the Job layout of your career site in Career Site Builder.
  4. Make sure the AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Resumes feature is enabled in Career Site Builder.

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are highly valued. Happy learning !!

More information:

Do you want to know how to extract AI-assisted Skills validation for job requisition? Check out the reference link below from SAP.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/c20bfb6bdee341f4aff1a057a3d46012.html?parentHref=/whats-new/8fcf4960eea24f78b1d7613da406a885?Product=Recruiting%26Version=2H%2B2024%26locale=en-US%23top&parentName=SAP+SuccessFactors+What%27s+New

SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Careers SAP Marketing

entry.title: ChangeSet - HTTP 403 (X-CSRF validation failed as X-REQUESTED-WITH request header is missing)
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a problem calling an Online ChangeSet request and I can't set the requested Header parameter X-REQUESTED-WITH because the ChangeSet Action do not have the Header Parameters Section.

ChangeSet:

{
    "_Type": "Action.Type.ODataService.ChangeSet",
    "ActionResult": {
        "_Name": "OnlineSimulatePricesCondition"
    },
    "OnFailure": "/xxx/Rules/CreateSalesOrder/ResultSimulatePricesConditionFail.js",
    "OnSuccess": "/xxx/Rules/CreateSalesOrder/ResultSimulatePricesConditionSuccess.js",
    "Target": {
        "Service": "/xxx/Services/ServiceV2_Online.service"
    },
    "Actions": [
        "/xxx/Actions/CreateSalesOrderActions/SalesOrderHeadOnline.action",
    ]
}

CreateSalesOrderHeadOnline.action:

{
    "_Type": "Action.Type.ODataService.CreateEntity",
    "ActionResult": {
        "_Name": "SalesOrderHead"
    },
    "Target": {
        "Service": "/xxx/Services/ServiceV2_Online.service",
        "EntitySet": "HeadSet"
    },
    "Properties": {
        "Ordertype": "#Page:CreateSalesOrderHeaderData/#Control:FCOrderType/#SelectedValue",
        "ShipTo": "#Page:CreateSalesOrderHeaderData/#Control:FCOrderShipTo/#Value",
        "OrderReason": "#Page:CreateSalesOrderHeaderData/#Control:FCOrderReason/#SelectedValue",
        "SoldTo": "#Page:CreateSalesOrderHeaderData/#Control:FCOrderSoldTo/#Value",
        "UM": "#Page:CreateSalesOrderHeaderData/#Control:FCUnitOfMeasure/#Value"
    },
    "Headers": {
        "X-REQUESTED-WITH": "X"
    }
}

 

The call fail with:

Error: HTTP status 403 (Forbidden); Error 403 (X-CSRF validation failed as X-REQUESTED-WITH request header is missing); POST https://xxx.hana.ondemand.com/s4-backend-odata/sap/opu/odata/sap/XXX/$batch

Others online OData Requests works well.

How i can resolve this issue ?

Thank you.

entry.title: Latest Features of Goal management in 2H/2024 Release cycle
entry.content:
1. Enhancements to AI-Assisted Writing and Availability in More Areas - We can now improve the content of performance and development goals while creating it. It includes various goal plan fields like name, metric, description. But this will apply only to goal fields of text and textarea types.

Two new options are available now which are Translate and Analyze Text. The 'Translate' option would help in converting the given text into any of the 146 languages(that particular language enablement from the provisioning end is required). Also, the 'Analyze Text' option would exclude inclusiveness and other harmful content from the fields.

Limitation : The AI-assisted writing tool isn't yet supported in custom learning activities which may be linked to 'Development Goals'. See more

2. Link and Unalign Goals - Now, the users can link their own goals to other user's goal and vice-versa. From his/her own goal plan, a user can select Link Goal to link to other user's goal plan. Also, a user can navigate to other user's goal plan and select Link with Mine to link it to their own goal plan. The linkage would also show on the Goal Alignment Chart for a better visibility. 

Also, there is an option to remove the linkage using Unalign Goal option as well. See more

3. Roll-up Calculation Supported for Cascaded and Linked Goals - When the rollup-calc-type attribute is true, now you can calculate sum or avg for the cascaded and linked goals in the bizx-actual, bizx-target and bizx-pos fields. Whenever any goal is cascaded or linked, it will generate two records in the audit trail. One would be 'Cascaded' or 'Linked', and the record would be 'Modified'.

As this link would be shown on the Goal Alignment Chart, it will enhance goal tracking of the linked and cascaded goals. See more

4. Hide "Create from Scratch" Option for Goal Creation - Previously, there were three fixed options to create goals on the goals page. Those are Create from Scratch, Copy from Goal Plan, Copy from Library. Now there is an option to hide the Create from Scratch option from the goals page. For this, the turnoff-add-personal-goal-button switch should be set to on in the goal plan template. See more

5. Multi-Line Display for Milestone Fields - In the milestone section of the goal details view, multi-line display is now supported for fields whose type is set to textarea, such as desc and actual. See more

6. Goal Modification Email Jobs Available in Admin Center - Earlier, we had many jobs not available in the admin center and we could only create them from provisioning end. A major 'Goal Modification Email' is now available in the admin center. In the job parameters, a new value, Past Week, is now available in the Job Parameters. See more

7. Latest Goal Management Allows Duplicate Assignment of Team Goals During Import - The Allow Duplicate Assignment of Team Goals feature is now available in the latest Goal Management. This feature is available only during goal import. See more

8. Copy Goals on Behalf of Others - In the latest goal management, managers have the option to navigate to his/her direct report's goal plan and copy the template from there. The goals created would then be visible in the user's goal plan. This is done so that the managers have the option to create a goal for their direct report on behalf of the employee. See more

9. User Experience Enhancements to the Latest Goal Management - These are as follows:

  1. Weights are now displayed for team goals on goal cards.
  2. Users can now include comments in the PDF output of goals.
  3. Users can now include inactive users in search results in People Selector.
  4. On the goal details page, we made the left-hand sections collapsible. Users can now choose the Show more button to expand the full content of milestone fields configured as the textarea type.
  5. On the goal details page, we moved the entry to audit history to a new section on the right hand.
  6. On the goal details page, we made all milestone fields visible without the need to choose the Manage Milestones button. The milestone table is now horizontally scrollable.
  7. Users can now choose the Show more button to expand the full content of milestone fields configured as the textarea type.
  8. In the Goal Alignment dialog, the column headers are updated to be more inclusive.
  9. Users can now search for assignees on the team goal details page.
  10. On the landing page, the tab names for performance and development goals are updated to use the plural form.
  11. More goals can now be displayed in goal list views.
  12. A new column Co-owner Username is now supported in both Story reports and Table reports for master team goals.
  13. In audit history, "Shared" and "Unshared" are now supported as new action types for team goals.  See more

10. Enhancements to AI-Assisted Goal Creation - Several enhancements to AI-assisted goal creation have been made in the latest Goal Management. See more

Thanks for reading. Let me know your insights in the comments below. 🙂

entry.title: How SAP manages its Early Talent programs – Get insights by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAPs Vocational Training (VT) department, responsible for training SAP’s apprentices and dual students, uses SAP SuccessFactors for its processes. However, there are apprentice and student specific processes not covered by SAP SuccessFactors, for which the VT department used custom solutions and many spreadsheets in the past. This caused redundancies, data flow gaps, a lack of transparency and a resource intensive reporting.

As dual students & apprentices ourselves, we wanted to solve those challenges and make our own lives easier, by developing a solution covering the unique requirements for early talent management and connecting it with the data from SAP SuccessFactors, providing a unified overview for students, trainers and managers alike.

Our internal Early Talent Management solution is built from the ground up on SAP Business Technology Platform, tightly integrated into SAP SuccessFactors, with now over 20 Fiori applications, covering most of the requirements of SAPs 14 VT programs from around the globe. We use the solution at SAP since mid 2022 and are currently evaluating if it could also be used to manage our interns.

With this Customer Engagement Initiative we’d like to share insights about the capabilities of our solution and evaluate if our internal solution might also be suitable for customer use cases in a potential future solution.

Form of activities

The final form of activities depends on the participants preferences. We currently plan:

  • Initial call (1h) in December 2024 to give an initial overview about the solutions capabilities as well as the timeline of the CEI project.
  • Throughout January till June 2025 deep dives (1-2h) into specific capability areas of the solution, including time for Q&A and feedback as well as discussing challenges you currently face. Our capability areas include:
    • Student & Cohort management
    • Work (Rotation) assignment & management
    • Conversion process for students into full-time positions
    • Academics & grade reporting by students
    • Extracurricular activities for students
    • Scientific papers & a small paper book library system
  • Furthermore, we’d like to offer two sessions (1-2h) focused on your current challenges in the following two areas to get a more holistic understanding of them:
    • Apprentice handling including the Apprentice report book (Berichtsheft)
    • Functionalities required for managing interns
  • Closing call (1h) in Q3 2025

Prerequisites

Customers and partners should use or plan to use SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and be familiar with apprenticeships, internships, co-op student or Vocational Training programs

Register for our CEI project ‘SAP Early Talent Management’ from October 14th till November 15th, 2024.

Once the registration process is finalized, we invite participants to the initial call to go through the project scope and timelines.

We are looking forward to meet you in December. 🙂
